title: Articles with place names
lesson: Most place names take no article: countries, cities, and continents (France, Tokyo, Africa). Use 'the' with plural or grouped names (the Netherlands, the USA, the Alps) and with oceans, rivers, and deserts (the Atlantic, the Nile, the Sahara).

items.text: {1} Nile is the longest river in Africa.
items.explanation: Rivers always take 'the' (the Nile, the Thames, the Amazon).

items.text: {1} Mount Everest is the highest peak in {2} Himalayas.
items.explanation: Individual mountains take no article (Mount Everest); mountain ranges take 'the' (the Himalayas).

items.text: {1} Amazon flows through {2} Brazil, one of {3} largest countries on Earth.
items.explanation: Rivers take 'the' (the Amazon); most countries take no article (Brazil); superlative phrases take 'the' (one of the largest).

items.text: She flew from {1} United States to {2} Netherlands.
items.explanation: Countries whose names are plural or contain 'States/Kingdom/Republic' take 'the' (the United States, the Netherlands).

items.text: He has always wanted to visit {1} Sahara Desert.
items.explanation: Deserts take 'the' (the Sahara Desert, the Gobi Desert).

items.text: They spent the summer island-hopping around {1} Philippines.
items.explanation: Groups of islands take 'the' (the Philippines, the Canary Islands, the Maldives) because the name is plural.

items.text: We sailed across {1} Pacific and then into {2} Mediterranean.
items.explanation: Oceans and seas take 'the' (the Pacific, the Atlantic, the Mediterranean, the Red Sea).

items.text: Although it is a large island, {1} Cyprus is much smaller than {2} Sicily.
items.explanation: Single islands take no article (Cyprus, Sicily, Madagascar) — unlike groups of islands, which take 'the'.

items.text: Our ship passed through {1} Suez Canal on its way to {2} Gulf.
items.explanation: Canals and gulfs take 'the' (the Suez Canal, the Panama Canal, the Gulf, the Gulf of Mexico).

items.text: Most reporters were stationed in {1} Middle East, covering {2} north of {3} Iraq.
items.explanation: Some regions take 'the' (the Middle East); 'the north/south of' + a country takes 'the' (the north of Iraq); but the country itself takes no article (Iraq).
